scores.ribbonTitle: The experiment is holding, but still under active test.
scores.note: Phase 2 scores are phase-aware and capped by stage maturity.

health.title: Commitment
health.copy: Entered capital is still staying in the structure, which supports the commitment thesis so far.

health.title: Distribution
health.copy: Participation remains relatively broad, which reduces early concentration risk.

health.title: Momentum
health.copy: Demand is still building with enough breadth to read the phase as active rather than exhausted.

health.title: Defensibility
health.copy: The raise profile is building a more credible path into TGE than a fragile sprint structure would.

timeline.title: Opening speculative round
timeline.copy: 10% of supply opened the experiment and established the base valuation anchor.

timeline.title: Full refund window
timeline.copy: Phase 1 participants could fully exit before the ladder stages began.

timeline.title: First ladder round
timeline.copy: 5% of supply at 1.5x the Phase 1 anchor. This phase sets the tone for ongoing commitment.

timeline.title: Defection-cost refund window
timeline.copy: Participants may still leave, but exits now pay the 27% defection cost.

timeline.title: Final speculative round
timeline.copy: Residual supply rolls into the final round, where the market reveals whether conviction remains.

narrative.heroSummary: The first ladder round is testing whether commitment can survive beyond the opening phase and continue to attract capital.
narrative.statusIntro: The live read is currently favorable: most core signals are still supporting the commitment model rather than undermining it.
narrative.statusDetail: Success depends on whether the structure keeps capital committed, sustains participation across phases, limits destructive concentration, and leaves behind a defendable launch setup.
narrative.statusWarning: So far there is no measurable exit wave. If that changes, the health layer will reflect it automatically.
